approach: Incremental 64-model labelled-primary CEGAR within the retained source-record-21 frozen order-30 boundary.

strategy.mechanism: Parse the immutable boundary CNF once, solve incrementally, and append the unique 426-literal clause falsified by each verified supplied-corpus primary vector.

hypothesis: Within 64 verified labelled completions of the frozen record-21 boundary, at least one valid Ramsey(5,5,42) graph is absent from the supplied 656 canonical classes.
discriminating_test: Enumerate at most 64 distinct primary vectors, require base-CNF and prior-block satisfaction plus two full graph checks per model, and accept novelty only after canonical nonmembership.

search_efficiency.naive_space: At most 2^426 boundary assignments after freezing 435 of the 861 graph edges; dominant costs are the 508157-clause SAT solve and independent full assignment/graph evaluation per model.
reductions_considered: incremental base parsing and learned-clause reuse
search_efficiency.chosen_mechanism: Keep the already audited fixed core and Hamming encoding, parse the base once with libcadical, append primary-only blocks, store total assignments as bitsets, and run complete semantic checks after every SAT result.
search_efficiency.estimated_or_measured_savings: 435/861 edge bits fixed (50.52%); 142506 core-only five-sets skipped; one 2.62 MB compressed base retained instead of about 168 MB for 64 copies; post-hoc sound row sorting reduced the 64 vectors to eight S_12 normal forms in this sample.
search_efficiency.soundness_guard: The cold checker independently reconstructed the primary map, evaluated 32522048 base clauses plus all ordered blocks, checked fixed-core equality and distance, enumerated maximal cliques in each graph and complement, and verified NetworkX isomorphism to nauty targets.

summary: The exact 64-model budget was reached. All 64 primary vectors were distinct and valid, but all landed in six supplied source classes: records 12, 18, 19, 20, 21, and 25. Distances were 136-226. The cold audit passed and found only eight destroy-permutation normal forms. No graph, class, boundary, or Ramsey bound was excluded.
rationale: The predeclared novelty signal did not occur. The independently measured 64-to-8 label redundancy explains why a larger arbitrary assignment budget has low information value and supplies a concrete, sound symmetry target for the next pass.
claims: The 64 retained primary vectors are distinct, satisfy the exact base and every earlier block, and decode to valid Ramsey(5,5,42) graphs in six supplied classes.
The frozen source-record-21 order-30 core has compatible completions in at least supplied source classes 12, 18, 19, 20, 21, and 25.
All 64 retained models have distinct destroyed-vertex core-neighborhood rows and collapse to eight normal forms under the destroy-label S_12 action.
evidence: Production command and limits in .proof-experiments/20260721-161659-700d02; cold audit in .proof-experiments/20260721-162745-91d1f9.
next_steps: Remove the source-specific Hamming constraint before quotienting, because it is not invariant under permutations of the destroyed labels.
Encode lexicographically nondecreasing 30-bit frozen-core rows for the 12 destroyed vertices and exhaustively verify orbit coverage on small distinct-row and tied-row controls.
Run one predeclared symmetry-normalized production first-model gate only after the coverage test passes.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260721-161659-700d02: 64 models in 609.167 seconds, 152472 KiB peak child RSS, no canonical nonmember.
.proof-experiments/20260721-162745-91d1f9: independent cold audit passed in 133.875 seconds, 79512 KiB peak child RSS.
.proof-experiments/20260721-164245-525153: exact two-host induced-core cost probe; the negative complement control took 10.631 seconds.
independent_checker: checkers/novel42_labelled_cegar_audit.py imports no producer. It independently maps primary edges, evaluates every base clause and prior block, validates graph/core/distance semantics, runs NetworkX clique scans, and verifies NetworkX isomorphism to the nauty-identified target. Production additionally required exact agreement between checkers/checker_a.py and the separately implemented checkers/checker_b.c.
transfer_insights: CEGAR assignment blocking -> if supplied rediscovery is driven by only a few labelled completions, 64 blocks should expose more classes or novelty -> 64 vectors reached only six supplied classes.

Symmetry quotienting -> if destroyed-label permutations dominate the loop, sorting their distinct core rows should merge many models -> the independently computed normal form reduced 64 vectors to eight.

established_facts.claim: The retained frozen order-30 core has compatible valid completions in at least six supplied source classes.
established_facts.evidence: Retained graph6 witnesses and independent target isomorphisms in artifacts/novel42_labelled_cegar64_cold_audit.json.
established_facts.scope: The six observed classes only; not a complete embedding census.

established_facts.claim: The 64 retained labelled primary vectors occupy eight destroy-permutation normal forms.
established_facts.evidence: Independent row reconstruction and normal-form hashes in artifacts/novel42_labelled_cegar64_cold_audit.json.
established_facts.scope: Exactly the retained 64 models, all of which have distinct core-neighborhood rows.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Continue the identical record-21 labelled-blocking loop with a larger arbitrary model cutoff.
ruled_out.scope: The immutable base, CaDiCaL incremental configuration, and one-primary-vector blocks.
ruled_out.reason: The declared 64 vectors yielded no novel class and collapsed to eight S_12 normal forms, while labelled blocks cannot exclude an isomorphism class.
ruled_out.evidence: Production and cold reports with the hashes above.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Install a proved class-level block or sound symmetry quotient, or demonstrate an artifact defect.

open_leads.description: Quotient the frozen boundary by sorting destroyed vertices on their frozen-core adjacency rows.
open_leads.rationale: Every completion has a row-sorted representative, and the current sample shows an exact 64-to-8 redundancy reduction when rows are distinct.
open_leads.next_experiment: Implement the lex CNF without the non-invariant Hamming constraint and exhaustively test small tied-row orbit coverage before one production solve.

blocker: One 426-literal clause excludes only one labelled vector; the run has no checked isomorphism-class block or exhaustive boundary certificate.
reopen_condition: A sound class-level block, an exhaustively validated boundary symmetry quotient, or a demonstrated source/mapping/checker defect.
reopen_evidence: The former need for replayable primary model blocking was satisfied by the 64-model incremental packet; it exposed label symmetry as the next blocker.

continuation.objective: Test a sound destroy-label quotient before granting more boundary-search budget.
continuation.first_action: Implement lex ordering of destroyed-vertex 30-bit core rows after removing the Hamming counter, then enumerate every small control orbit including tied rows to verify coverage.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop on a coverage defect, a checker discrepancy, one dual-checked supplied-corpus novel graph, or the predeclared normal-form budget.

campaign_assessment.close_signal: A measured 64-to-8 redundancy reduction identifies a concrete sound quotient target; it is not evidence that the exact Ramsey value is near resolution.
campaign_assessment.reason: One bounded pass remains information-rich because it tests a proved group action rather than enlarging an arbitrary cutoff.

strategy_proposals.mechanism: Remove the source Hamming counter and impose lex order on the destroyed vertices' frozen-core rows, with explicit tied-row coverage controls.
strategy_proposals.hypothesis: The quotient avoids the measured label redundancy and changes the first-model supplied-class distribution.
strategy_proposals.discriminating_test: Exhaustive small orbit-coverage gate followed by one predeclared production first model.
strategy_proposals.rationale: It targets the exact 64-to-8 redundancy measured by the cold audit and has a direct coverage proof by sorting.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: Can exact labelled blocking within one known order-42 frozen boundary reach a supplied-corpus-novel Ramsey graph?
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: Extends the prior one-model boundary check to a replayable 64-model incremental ledger and diagnoses an eight-normal-form symmetry bottleneck.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: Accept only the exact 64-model/cold-audit scope and retained corpus-local witnesses; do not accept a class exclusion, exhaustive boundary result, or Ramsey bound.

closest_prior_work.difference: The authoritative source supplies the 328 graphs and complements but not this frozen-core completion ledger.
The upper-bound work establishes R(5,5) <= 46; this bounded order-42 search neither strengthens nor reproduces that bound.

novelty_searches.source: Dynamic Survey of Small Ramsey Numbers, revision 18
novelty_searches.query: R(5,5), order-42 supplied graphs, and current bound
novelty_searches.finding: The exact value remains open and the supplied order-42 graphs are evidence, not a complete classification.

external_channel.acceptance_path: No external submission is justified for this bounded internal route diagnostic.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol was principal investigator. Supplied GPT-5.6 Terra literature-strategy and experiment-verification delegates were advisory and promoted with provenance; Sol audited their claims and no new subagent was spawned. Deterministic tools were Python 3.12.3, GCC/G++ 13.3.0, CaDiCaL library 1.7.4, nauty labelg, NetworkX 3.3, gzip, SHA-256, and the computational-researcher experiment harness. Ubuntu libcadical-dev 1.7.4-1 was installed and recorded. No CAS, proof assistant, external publication, or remote account was used.
